chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special Sales Tax Reference Application 135 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.26 2. For hearing of main case 31.10.2025 Mr. Muhammad Bilal, advocate holds brief for Mr. Aqeel Ahmed Khan, advocate for the applicant On the last date following order was passed: 1. To be satisfied before the next date. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Counsel for the applicant has confronted as to the order of the Tribunal, which primarily appears to be in favour of the Applicant, Counsel needs time to seek instructions. Time allowed. Adjourned.” Today once again brief is being held and adjournment sought for no cogent reason whatsoever. It appears that the applicant is not interested in proceeding with the matter. In view of judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of C.I.R. vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 47 subsection 5 of Sales Tax Act, 1990. Judge Judge Amjad
